meaning: vt.使没有充分发挥作用,扣着大牌不出而出小牌,表演有克制,对...轻描淡写,表演不充分vi.表演角色不充分n.秘密行动,不充分的表演
examples: We often underplay the skills we have.
I do not mean to gloss over or underplay the obstacles and needs.
Opinion polls underplay Mr Le Pen's support; almost half of respondents say they are undecided.
Is it sometimes worse to underplay potential hazards and consequences than to err on the side of caution?
That means to make its products cool, Microsoft has to often underplay its name, in products like XBox and Halo.
